best_agent_match.title: ETH sent for WETH-backed mints is wrapped into Revest and never forwarded to the vault

findings.title: Fee-on-transfer tokens can mint or top up FNFTs with less collateral than accounting assumes

best_agent_match.title: Fee-on-transfer tokens can mint or top up FNFTs with less collateral than accounting assumes

findings.title: Address-lock mints accept non-compliant trigger addresses and can permanently lock funds

best_agent_match.title: Address-lock mints do not reject non-compliant trigger contracts

rejected_candidates.title: Integer Overflow in ERC20 Fee Calculation
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected because the contracts compile with Solidity `^0.8.0`, so arithmetic overflow reverts rather than silently wrapping.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing Underflow Protection in FNFT Burn
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected because any underflow on `supply[id] -= amount` reverts in Solidity 0.8; this is not an exploitable accounting bug by itself.

rejected_candidates.title: Address Lock Can Block Legitimate Unlocks
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected as stated because choosing a malicious lock implementation is user-controlled behavior; the reportable protocol issue is the separate accepted finding that non-compliant triggers are accepted without validation.

rejected_candidates.title: Value Lock Oracle Price Manipulation
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected because the provided code only stores a caller-supplied oracle address and contains no oracle implementation or specific manipulable pricing logic to support the flash-loan claim.

rejected_candidates.title: Split Function Burns FNFT Without Proportional Asset Distribution
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected as speculative because the proportional distribution logic lives in the unseen `TokenVault` implementation; the supplied source does not support this claim.

rejected_candidates.title: No Access Control Check on extendFNFTMaturity
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected because `extendFNFTMaturity()` requires `balance == supply`, so only an account holding the entire outstanding supply can extend maturity.

rejected_candidates.title: Unchecked Return Value from External Call
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected because `IAddressLock.createLock()` has no return value; failures revert rather than returning an unchecked status.

rejected_candidates.title: Potential Division by Zero
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected because `erc20multiplierPrecision` is a hard-coded constant `1000`, so the division-by-zero scenario is unsupported.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing Zero Address Validation for Asset
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected because `asset == address(0)` is explicitly treated as a special case by skipping the ERC20 transfer path; the claimed unsafe `safeTransferFrom(address(0), ...)` path is not present.

rejected_candidates.title: Race Condition in ERC20 Approval
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected because granting a standing allowance to the configured rewards handler is a trust/configuration choice, not a concrete protocol exploit in the provided code.

rejected_candidates.title: Inconsistent fnftsCreated Counter
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected as a standalone issue because the counter increments once per new series as intended in normal flow; the real exploitability is the accepted reentrancy/id-reuse finding.

rejected_candidates.title: depositAdditionalToFNFT Allows Partial Deposit Without Proportional Fee
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected because the cited code computes the ERC20 fee directly from `quantity * amount`; no concrete under-collection path for new-series deposits is shown.

rejected_candidates.title: No Validation of endTime in mintTimeLock
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected because allowing an already-mature time lock is at most a user-footgun and does not create realistic protocol-level harm by itself.

rejected_candidates.title: Potential Griefing via Non-Transferable FNFTs
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected because `nontransferrable` is an explicit FNFT configuration choice, not an unintended permissionless exploit.
